title: JSON Configuration Parser and Validator Module
summary: A Python module that parses and validates JSON configuration files against a defined schema. The module should provide clear error messages for invalid configurations and support flexible schema definitions.

user_stories.title: Parse a valid JSON configuration file
user_stories.description: As a developer, I want to parse a valid JSON configuration file so that I can access its contents programmatically.
acceptance_criteria: The module reads a JSON file from the file system
The module successfully parses the JSON content into a Python dictionary
No exceptions are raised for valid JSON

user_stories.title: Validate JSON configuration against a schema
user_stories.description: As a developer, I want to validate a JSON configuration file against a schema so that I can ensure it meets required structure and constraints.
acceptance_criteria: The module supports schema definition using JSON Schema format
The module checks the JSON configuration against the provided schema
The module returns a boolean indicating whether the configuration is valid

user_stories.title: Provide detailed validation errors
user_stories.description: As a developer, I want to receive detailed error messages when a configuration is invalid so that I can quickly fix issues.
acceptance_criteria: The module returns a list of validation errors
Each error includes the path to the invalid field
Each error includes a descriptive message explaining the issue

user_stories.title: Support custom schema validation rules
user_stories.description: As a developer, I want to define custom validation rules so that I can enforce specific business logic on the configuration.
acceptance_criteria: The module allows users to register custom validation functions
Custom validation functions are executed during the validation process
Validation errors from custom rules are included in the output

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The module should handle large JSON files efficiently without excessive memory usage.
The module should gracefully handle malformed JSON and provide meaningful error messages.
The module should be easily extendable to support additional validation rules or schema formats.
The module should include comprehensive documentation for all public APIs and usage examples.

constraints: The module must be compatible with Python 3.8 and above
The module must not introduce external dependencies beyond standard libraries
assumptions: The input JSON files will be well-formed and follow standard JSON syntax
Schema definitions will be provided in JSON Schema format
Users will have basic knowledge of Python and JSON
out_of_scope: Support for non-JSON configuration formats (e.g., YAML, INI)
Integration with external systems or APIs
